meta.price_note: Typical street prices. Ranges [low,high] where vendors or batches differ. EUR prices converted approximately, marked in notes.

vga-rgb.name: Analog RGB (VGA)
vga-rgb.explain: RGB over a VGA/DB15 connector, for PVMs, SCART CRTs, upscalers.

ypbpr.name: Component (YPbPr)
ypbpr.explain: Component video for consumer CRTs and some TVs.

yc.name: Y/C (S-Video)
yc.explain: Separated luma/chroma via MikeS11's encoder work. A usable, filtered Y/C signal, not just the raw source.

yc-active-filtered.name: Actively filtered Y/C
yc-active-filtered.explain: Y/C through a THS7374-class amplifier stage with NTSC-spec voltage correction, the cleanest chain.

yc-source.name: Y/C source signal
yc-source.explain: Internal token: a VGA-pin signal a Y/C adapter can filter. Provided by an analog I/O board or a Direct Video DAC.

yc-source-io.name: Y/C source from analog I/O board
yc-source-io.explain: Internal token: the MikeS11 ACTIVE adapter only accepts this (needs 5V on VGA pin 9, Sync-on-Green off); it rejects Direct Video sources.

sdram128.name: 128MB SDRAM
sdram128.explain: Core-side SDRAM. Needed by many cores; 128MB is the de facto standard (big Neo Geo ROMs, Mega CD, GBA).

dual-sdram.name: Dual 128MB SDRAM
dual-sdram.explain: Two modules; needed for Saturn and other dual-RAM cores.

sdram-slot.name: SDRAM socket
sdram-slot.explain: Internal token: accepts an SDRAM module.

gpio-header.name: GPIO expansion header
gpio-header.explain: Internal token: accepts the I/O-board addon stack.

user-io-port.name: USER I/O port
user-io-port.explain: Internal token: accepts SNAC controller adapters.

usb-otg-header.name: USB OTG header
usb-otg-header.explain: Internal token: accepts the USB hub addon.

usb-hub.name: Multiple USB ports
usb-hub.explain: Enough USB for controllers, keyboard, WiFi, etc. without a squid of adapters.

ethernet.name: Wired Ethernet
ethernet.explain: For updates, game streaming from NAS, remote control.

snac.name: SNAC (native controller ports)
snac.explain: Original console controllers wired near-directly into the core; lowest possible input lag.

nfc.name: NFC game loading
nfc.explain: Tap a card/figure to launch a game (Zaparoo-style).

cd-drive.name: Optical disc drive
cd-drive.explain: Load real CDs (PS1 now; Saturn/Sega CD support maturing in firmware).

m2-slot.name: M.2 SSD bay
m2-slot.explain: Internal storage beyond the SD card.

jamma.name: JAMMA edge
jamma.explain: Drops into a real arcade cabinet as the game PCB.

usbc-power.name: USB-C power
usbc-power.explain: Powered by any decent USB-C phone charger; no barrel brick.

barrel-power.name: 5V barrel-jack power
barrel-power.explain: Internal token: needs a 5V DC barrel supply.

boot-media.name: Boot SD card
boot-media.explain: Internal token: SD card with the MiSTer OS.

psu.name: Power supply
psu.explain: Internal token: an adequate power source.

case.name: Enclosure
case.explain: Internal token: a case around the board stack.

controller.name: A controller
controller.explain: Internal token: any USB gamepad to actually play.

midi-mt32.name: MT-32 MIDI synth
midi-mt32.explain: Roland MT-32/SoundFont music for DOS-era cores via mt32-pi.

handheld.name: Handheld
handheld.explain: A MiSTer you can hold. Nothing orderable provides this yet.

lanes.name: Turnkey consoles
lanes.tagline: Plug in and play. No screwdriver.

lanes.name: Assembled kits
lanes.tagline: The classic stack, built and tested by someone else.

lanes.name: Build your own
lanes.tagline: A bare board plus the addons you choose.

lanes.name: Special builds
lanes.tagline: Cabinets and consoles with their own boards.

lanes.name: On the horizon
lanes.tagline: Announced. Not orderable. Do not wait for these unless you enjoy waiting.

sdram-128.name: 128MB SDRAM module
sdram-128.notes: The de facto standard module; 32MB is largely phased out of retail. Many popular cores need SDRAM at all, so treat this as part of a real build, not an extra.

sdram-128-second.name: Second 128MB SDRAM module (dual RAM)
sdram-128-second.notes: Dual-RAM configs (Saturn, future PSX work) need two modules and an I/O board that supports the second socket (e.g. IO Direct).

io-analog.name: Analog I/O board (IO Analog Pro / official v6-class)
io-analog.notes: The board that turns a bare MiSTer into a CRT machine: VGA RGB or YPbPr by cable choice, audio, fan header, second SD. A EUR349.90 price seen in one search result is a stale-snippet artifact (that is a full built unit's price), not this board's.

io-digital.name: Digital I/O board (v1.2)
io-digital.notes: S/PDIF digital audio out (96kHz); DE10-Nano SW3 must be ON. Current price unverified: the only figure found (EUR349.90) duplicates the analog board snippet and is not trusted.

usb-hub.name: USB hub addon (v2.1)
usb-hub.notes: 7 ports (1 power-only + 6 USB 2.0), FE2.1 chip; needs its own power via rear barrel splitter (included).

direct-video-dac.name: Direct Video HDMI DAC (generic AG6200-class dongle)
direct-video-dac.notes: Firmware feature (direct_video=1) plus a cheap HDMI-to-VGA dongle: analog RGB without an I/O board, same low latency. While enabled the HDMI port stops being normal HDMI. Some gear wants a 470-ohm inline resistor on sync.

yc-passive.name: MikeS11 Y/C adapter, PASSIVE
yc-passive.notes: Passive luma-trap filter, no amplifier. Explicitly dual-compatible: analog I/O board VGA (direct_video=0) OR Direct Video DAC (direct_video=1). The flexible budget path to S-Video.

yc-active.name: MikeS11 Y/C adapter, ACTIVE (THS7374)
yc-active.vendor: Ultimate MiSTer (EUR24.90 ~ $27); MiSTer Addons YC Active Encoder Board ($55)
yc-active.notes: THS7374 amplifier, NTSC-spec voltage modes, luma trap for clean composite. Ultimate MiSTer's own page contradicts itself on Direct Video: the description mentions a DV mode but the compatibility instructions say NOT compatible with Direct Video dongles and require the analog I/O board's VGA with 5V on pin 9 and Sync-on-Green off. We model the restrictive reading; flagged, not resolved. EARMARK 2026-07-16: site owner recalls getting Y/C over a Direct Video HDMI-VGA dongle successfully - consistent with the PASSIVE adapter (documented DV-compatible); whether the ACTIVE adapter also works on DV despite its compatibility notes still needs bench verification.

snac-adapter.name: SNAC / SNAX / SNAX64 adapter
snac-adapter.vendor: MiSTer Addons ($15 SNAC V1, $35 SNAX V2.1 adds PSX/NeoGeo+2P, $50 SNAX64 adds 4P N64); Retro Remake bundles $5-10
snac-adapter.notes: Plugs into the USER I/O header, not the I/O board. PSX and N64 controllers need SNAX/SNAX64 tiers, not SNAC V1. Console cables usually sold separately.

rtc-board.name: RTC board
rtc-board.notes: Cheap, niche: few cores use it, auto-set anyway if networked.

mt32-pi-kit.name: MT32-pi interface kit
mt32-pi-kit.vendor: various (~$24 for the interface; Raspberry Pi 3+ sold separately)
mt32-pi-kit.notes: Roland MT-32 / SoundFont synth for DOS-era cores; budget the Pi on top.

case-armor.name: Aluminum Armor case (passive cooling)
case-armor.notes: Whole case is the heatsink; fanless. Fits DE10-Nano-class boards incl. MiSTer Pi with an IO Analog Pro / IO Direct board.

case-acrylic.name: Acrylic case
case-acrylic.notes: Cheapest enclosure; vented for a fan.

psu-barrel-5a.name: 5V 5A barrel power supply
psu-barrel-5a.notes: The recommended upgrade over stock 2A-class adapters once addons hang off the board.

sd-card.name: MicroSD card (32GB+, U1/Class 10)
sd-card.notes: SanDisk Ultra class is the community default.

usb-controller.name: Any USB gamepad
usb-controller.notes: 8BitDo-class pads are the community default; Bluetooth works where the option provides it.

superdock.name: SuperDock
superdock.notes: SuperStation One only. Tray-loading DVD-RW, unpopulated M.2 2280 bay, 4 more USB-A, IR remote. Preorder, sold out; disc loading works for PS1, Saturn/Sega CD support still maturing in firmware. Shipped later than the original window.

options.name: Retro Remake SuperStation One
options.notes: PSone-shaped complete MiSTer: 128MB RAM, ADV7125 24-bit DAC, HDMI up to 1440p-class, VGA/component/composite/S-Video plus a Saturn-LOOKING 10-pin mini-DIN that is NOT pin-compatible with real Saturn cables. PS1 SNAC controller+memory-card ports, NFC loading, 64GB card preinstalled. Y/C is native, no dongle needed; DIP switch 3 picks FPGA MikeS-style encoding vs an onboard Sony encoder chip (forum consensus: FPGA path better). Whether the built-in path has THS7374-class active filtering is UNVERIFIED by any teardown; early firmware had mono/color-banding glitches on some cores, since patched. RTC needs a user-installed CR1220. No PSU in box. Sells in batches; sold out between batches (Batch 4 gone as of July 2026). Wired Ethernet claimed in one review, absent from vendor specs: unverified.

options.name: MiSTer Addons Aluminum Armor Bundle
options.notes: The classic full stack, fanless aluminum, I/O board style chosen at purchase (Analog Pro / IO Direct / VGA; IO Direct config ships dual RAM). 32GB card preloaded. Sold out at check ('back in soon'). USB-PD Reflex Volt supply in the USB-C configs.

options.name: MiSTer Addons Skeleton Kit
options.notes: Same stack, bare by design. Current stock status unverified (search-sourced).

options.name: Ultimate MiSTer PRO
options.notes: EUR349.90 excl VAT. Genuine DE10-Nano (explicitly not a clone), AV Pro 9.2 board, official case, assembled and tested. DUAL RAM tier at ~EUR379.90 adds second module + Digital I/O + 24-bit Direct Video for Saturn-class cores; BlisSTer tier (~EUR419.90) swaps in the low-latency BlisSTer hub, HDMI-only, no VGA.

options.name: Ultimate MiSTer QMTech Build
options.notes: EUR199.90 excl VAT: the budget assembled route, built on the QMTech clone with a matched I/O board and case. Cheapest 'someone else built it' path found.

options.name: iCode Fully Built MiSTer
options.notes: In stock at check, which is rare in this market. Price tiers by SD size (64GB-1TB). Digital I/O build: no analog video out of the box. 6A supply, RGB fan, RTC board loose in box. Dual-RAM upgrade available.

options.name: Terasic DE10-Nano
options.notes: The reference board. Chronically scarce since the chip shortage; often only reliably available direct from Terasic (ships a 5V/2.5A adapter, upgrade advised once addons stack up). At $225 bare it is hard to justify against the MiSTer Pi unless you specifically want the original. July-2026 stock status not freshly confirmed.

options.name: Retro Remake MiSTer Pi
options.notes: The board that broke the DE10 price. Same Cyclone V class, 1GB DDR3, USB-C power, accessory-compatible dimensions/headers. Sold in preorder batches (price drifts $99-125 by batch/bundle; $160 Mega Pack adds hub + A/V board, ~$180 full case kit). Sold out between batches. Own accessory line: A/V Pro 9.2 $40, 128MB SDRAM $20, hub $20, RTC $5, acrylic case $10.

options.name: QMTech Cyclone V Clone
options.notes: Automotive-grade Cyclone V with a higher thermal ceiling than the DE10's chip: the fanless builder's pick, and actually in stock when others aren't. Some variants have SDRAM integrated on-board (+~$15; then skip the module). Caveats: RAM slot orientation differs from DE10 on one header (people have fried modules inserting them the DE10 way) and QC is more variable than Terasic.

options.name: MiSTer Addons MiSTercade V2
options.notes: Your MiSTer becomes the JAMMA PCB in a real cabinet: 24-bit video via dedicated RGB DAC, adjustable 3.3V JAMMA edge, 13.5W mono amp, GP2040-CE sub-1ms controls, 8 buttons/player. Lite kit (BYO DE10 + RAM) ~$275. Sold out at check ('back in soon'). Cabinet not included, obviously.

options.name: Heber MiSTer Multisystem2
options.notes: Not a DE10 carrier: one integrated motherboard (own Cyclone V, 128MB SDRAM + 1GB LPDDR, powered 7-port hub) in a 3D-printed enclosure, assembled. ~$300 shipped HDMI-only; ~+$50 for the analog-video version (24-bit, 9-pin mini-DIN RGB+sync for SCART). GBP264 inc VAT in the UK. Shipping now, 1-2 week lead. JAMMA 'Arcade' edition preorders ship from Aug 2026. Enclosure files are open (Printables, CC-BY-SA); Multisystem1 is fully open-source and discontinued as a product.

options.name: Taki Udon Agilex 5 boards ('MiSTer 2')
options.vendor: Taki Udon / Retro Remake, with Altera
options.notes: Engineering boards in MiSTer Pi form factor being seeded to developers; no consumer product or date. Stated target 1.5-3x MiSTer Pi pricing. Motivation: Cyclone V is out of headroom for full N64/Saturn accuracy; Agilex 5 could reach toward Dreamcast-class. Cores must be ported before any of this plays games.

options.name: Heber Multisystem2 Pocket
options.notes: Handheld Multisystem2 in open community-input development; 4:3 screen, modular controls planned; 'before end of 2026' aspiration, no committed date or price. The widescreen 'Wide Boy' prototype seen in press is explicitly NOT the final design. Only credible MiSTer handheld project found; no orderable MiSTer portable exists.

options.name: Terasic DE25-Nano
options.notes: The board is real and orderable ($248; ~25% more logic, DDR4, much faster ARM) but MiSTer does NOT run on it: no official framework port confirmed as of July 2026. Listed so nobody buys one expecting a MiSTer. A likely future host, nothing more yet.

options.name: MARS FPGA
options.notes: CANCELLED November 2025. Was pitched as a DE10-eclipsing open board (Dreamcast/NAOMI/3DO/Jaguar ambitions, 4K + full analog out). Kept here so the landscape says so out loud instead of letting old hype linger.

appendix_not_mister.name: Analogue Pocket / Analogue 3D
appendix_not_mister.one_liner: Closed platform with its own 'openFPGA' core format; polished, but not MiSTer's cores, updates, or ecosystem (the 3D is N64-only, no openFPGA).

appendix_not_mister.name: ModRetro Chromatic
appendix_not_mister.one_liner: A single-system FPGA Game Boy/GBC replica ($199+), cartridge-based; no relation to MiSTer.

appendix_not_mister.name: ModRetro M64
appendix_not_mister.one_liner: A dedicated N64-compatible FPGA console ($229.99, launching July 28, 2026, AMD Artix UltraScale+, real cartridges); single-system, not MiSTer.

appendix_not_mister.name: FPGBC (FunnyPlaying)
appendix_not_mister.one_liner: A budget FPGA Game Boy Color clone handheld/kit; single-system, not MiSTer.

appendix_not_mister.name: Replay2 (FPGAArcade)
appendix_not_mister.one_liner: An in-development Agilex 5 mini-ITX FPGA machine from the FPGAArcade lineage; same next-gen conversation, different core ecosystem, not MiSTer-compatible.

interview.q: How much building do you want to do?

answers.label: None - ready out of the box
I want to build it myself

interview.q: What are you plugging it into?
Original console controllers, plugged in natively?

answers.label: A shelf, a desk, under the TV
I can wait for a batch or preorder
